task_completion.visual_evidence: The Juris-M (or Zotero-like) application window is open and visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Locate the 'Research' collection in the left pane.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The collection named 'Research' is visible in the left sidebar/pane of the Juris-M interface.

task_completion.description: Initiate the rename action for the collection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A context menu is open on the 'Research' collection with 'Rename Collection...' selected, or a rename dialog box is visible.
The text 'First Amendment Jurisprudence' is typed into the collection rename dialog box.

task_completion.description: Confirm the collection has been renamed successfully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The left sidebar now displays a collection named 'First Amendment Jurisprudence' instead of 'Research'.

task_completion.description: Ensure the items in the collection are preserved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: With 'First Amendment Jurisprudence' selected in the left pane, the middle pane displays legal items (e.g., US Supreme Court cases or law review articles).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Juris-M graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show mouse clicks, context menus, and dialog boxes within the Juris-M application.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to modify the database directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are open showing sqlite3 commands, python scripts, or bash scripts modifying the zotero.sqlite database.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual Juris-M software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application shown is Juris-M (which visually resembles Zotero), not a text editor or mock interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target application is Juris-M (Jurism), a legal reference manager with a UI similar to Zotero. The original collection name is 'Research' and the target new name is 'First Amendment Jurisprudence'. The collection contains real US Supreme Court cases (e.g., Brown v. Board of Education, Marbury v. Madison) and law review articles. The VLM should verify the rename operation in the GUI and ensure the legal items are still visible in the middle pane when the new collection is selected.
